Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild renal impairment (elevated creatinine), hypoglycemia with low HbA1c, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has low specific gravity. These findings suggest possible diabetic nephropathy or another renal pathology, with poor glycemic control and possible hepatic involvement. Further clinical correlation and nephrology/hepatology evaluation are recommended.
